title: Daily Market Digest: Geopolitical Tensions & Tech Rout Weigh on Markets
subtitle: Wednesday, July 8, 2026

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions and Tech Sell-Off Drag Down U.S. Markets
items.summary: U.S. equities closed lower on Wednesday, with major indices experiencing declines amidst escalating geopolitical tensions between the U.S. and Iran, which sent oil prices surging. A continued rout in semiconductor and AI-related stocks further dampened market sentiment. Investors also closely watched the release of the Federal Reserve's June FOMC minutes for insights into monetary policy.

items.headline: Crinetics Pharmaceuticals Surges on Strong Performance
items.summary: Crinetics Pharmaceuticals Inc. ($CRNX) posted a significant one-day gain of +98.7%. The stock has been a top performer in July, showing strong momentum.

items.headline: Cognizant Technology Solutions Rallies on Google Cloud Partnership
items.summary: Cognizant Technology Solutions Corp. ($CTSH) saw its shares rally 6.2% following news of an expanded partnership with Google Cloud for enterprise adoption of Gemini AI.

items.headline: Elicio Therapeutics Plunges After Direct Offering Pricing
items.summary: Elicio Therapeutics, Inc. ($ELTX) experienced a sharp decline of -37.35% after announcing the pricing of a $15 million registered direct offering.

items.headline: Rivian Automotive Shares Fall on Stock Sale Decision
items.summary: Rivian Automotive Inc. ($RIVN) shares plunged 18.1% after the company decided to sell 75 million shares of its Class A common stock.

items.headline: Semiconductor Stocks Hit by Broader Sector Rout
items.summary: Advanced Micro Devices ($AMD) sank 6.5% as the semiconductor sector continued its downturn, fueled by concerns over AI valuations and competitive pressures.

items.headline: SpaceX ($SPCX) Dips on Nasdaq 100 Debut
items.summary: SpaceX ($SPCX) fell 6.8% on its first day of trading after being included in the Nasdaq 100 index, amidst a broader downturn for AI-related tech stocks.

items.headline: Healthcare Sector Shows Relative Strength Amidst Market Weakness
items.summary: The healthcare sector continues to demonstrate significant relative strength, with 91% of S&P 500 healthcare stocks outperforming the broader market over the past month. Financials are also showing concentrated strength, particularly in Asset Management & Custody Banks and Multi-Family Residential REITs. Energy stocks saw a premarket lift due to surging oil prices.

items.headline: Technology and Semiconductor Sectors Experience Rout
items.summary: The technology sector, particularly semiconductors, faced a significant downturn. The Nasdaq 100 saw a sharp decline, with a renewed sell-off attributed to factors like Samsung's earnings miss and new AI chip development, questioning the sustainability of AI hyperscaler spending.

items.headline: Fed Releases June FOMC Minutes, Reveals Divided Committee
items.summary: The Federal Reserve released the minutes from its June 17 FOMC meeting today at 2:00 p.m. ET. The committee had unanimously kept the federal funds rate steady at 3.50%–3.75%, but the minutes are being scrutinized for insights into individual members' views on inflation and the economic effects of the Middle East conflict. Nine out of 19 policymakers expect at least one more rate hike before year-end 2026.

items.headline: U.S.-Iran Tensions Spike, Driving Oil Prices Higher
items.summary: Geopolitical tensions between the U.S. and Iran intensified after President Donald Trump declared an interim ceasefire 'over' following U.S. strikes on Iran. This development led to a surge of over 5% in oil prices, contributing to renewed inflation worries and impacting global markets.

items.headline: Equifax Report Highlights Growing Pressure on U.S. Middle Class
items.summary: Equifax released its first quarter 2026 Market Pulse Index, which declined for the second consecutive quarter, from 61.6 to 60.9. The report indicates an accelerated movement of consumers towards economic extremes, highlighting a 'K-Shaped economy' where the middle class is contracting.

items.headline: Major Earnings Reports on the Horizon
items.summary: While Levi Strauss A. ($LEVI), PriceSmart ($PSMT), and AZZ ($AZZ) were expected to report earnings today, major banks JPMorgan Chase and Goldman Sachs are scheduled to release their Q2 earnings on July 14. OneMain Holdings ($OMF) will report on July 29, and Flagstar Bank, N.A. ($FLG) on July 24.
